title: Subtractive GCD (Euclid's original, no division) = Nat.gcd
statement_informal: Euclid's original algorithm used repeated subtraction, no division or remainder: gcd(a,b) = gcd(a-b,b) for a >= b (and symmetrically). subgcd implements exactly that with a fuel bound a+b. Correctness: subgcd fuel a b = Nat.gcd a b for a+b <= fuel. Contrast euclid-gcd, which uses mod.

cost_model.notes: Each step subtracts the smaller from the larger; a+b strictly decreases, so a+b steps suffice. Correctness via Nat.gcd_sub_diag_r and Nat.gcd_comm.

references: Euclid's original subtraction-based algorithm (Elements VII). Subtraction-only contrast to work-units/euclid-gcd (which uses mod).
